evidence_excerpt: At the last examination 42 months after transplantation, the patient was asymptomatic with unremarkable clinical examination and imaging (including 3 h 18-FDG PET/CT) and undetectable (Em2+) or weak and declining (Em18) serology levels; oral albendazole was uninterrupted and dose-adjusted, with ongoing long-term monitoring planned.

quality_of_life.functional_status_evidence_excerpt: Patient was asymptomatic at 42 months and continued professional activities as a veterinarian; clinical exam and imaging were unremarkable.
quality_of_life.symptom_burden_evidence_excerpt: At 42 months post-transplant, the patient was asymptomatic with normal imaging and declining/undetectable serologies.

timeline_events.evidence_excerpt: Hepatectomy and right adrenalectomy were performed 15 months post-transplantation with free resection margins.
At 42 months after transplantation, the patient was asymptomatic with unremarkable clinical examination and imaging, and declining/undetectable serology.

notes_on_uncertainty: Index time approximated as the date of definitive AE diagnosis (~12 months post-transplantation) when imaging, biopsy, and serology confirmed AE and albendazole was initiated. Subsequent events are mapped relative to this approximation (surgery at ~3 months after diagnosis; asymptomatic status at ~30 months after diagnosis). Functional status inference is based on asymptomatic status and continued work; precise activity level not quantified.
